title: Cost-efficient Yeast genome Flongle library
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"chbso","text":"A cost-efficient protocol for constructing a Flongle library.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cgqfm","text":"PEG/NaCl precipitation buffer (see Rocky Mountain adventure protocol)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egcvf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1l34","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"bnfe8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5p99v","text":"PEG wash buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2av36","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hrdh","text":"Dilute PEG/NaCl precipitation buffer with the same amount of water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":["116",100],"data":[["PEG 8000","9% (w/v)"],["NaCl","1 M"],["Tris-HCl (pH 8.0)","10 mM"]],"guid":"BD0EA6D07CF111EDA794BDD7F9E714F2","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["PEG 8000","9% (w/v)"],["NaCl","1 M"],["Tris-HCl (pH 8.0)","10 mM"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23]}}}}
steps: Assemble the following components to end repair and add A to the genomic sample: A B 1 Ultra II buffer 1.75 2 End repair buffer 1.75 3 Ultra II enzyme 1 4 End repair enzyme 1 5 DNA x (500 ng) 6 MQ 44.5 - x Incubate at 20 °C for 00:05:00 , then 65 °C for 00:05:00 .

Add 50 µL of Ampure beads (or equivalent beads) and mix well. Wash with 75 % EtOH twice and elute with 11 µL of 10 millimolar (mM) Tris-HCl (pH 8.0). Keep the tube on the magnetic rack and do not disturb the beads while washing with 75% EtOH. Use 1 µL of the solution to check the concentration with Qubit. The concentration needs to be at least 30 ng/uL .

Assemble the following components for ligation: A B 1 DNA 10 uL 2 TLB 4 uL 3 Quick ligase 1.5 uL 4 AMX 0.8 uL Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Add 2.3 µL of 5 Molarity (M) NaCl and incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 and then cfg at max speed for 00:20:00 . Remove sup and add PEG wash buffer, cfg at max speed for 00:01:00 . Remove sup and add 11 µL of 10mM Tris-HCl (pH 8.0) and incubate at 4 °C for Overnight . Check the concentration with Qubit.
Assemble the following priming solution: A B 1 FLB 2.5 uL 2 FB 97.5 uL and the library mix solution: A B 1 DNA x uL (100 ng) 2 SB II 10 uL 3 LB II 4 uL 4 MQ 6 - x uL . Load them and start sequencing.
creator: Yutaro Hori
